summary: End-to-end handoff test from Hermes to Kublai. If this appears in Telegram, the internal mirror path is operational.
detail: Synthetic test entry created by Kublai at Danny request to verify the handoff mirror.

summary: Hermes helper script test: shared append tool works and should be mirrored by Kublai.
detail: Generated via append_handoff.py to verify Hermes has a simple write path.

summary: Danny added a protocol requirement: before executing shared work, Hermes and Kublai should coordinate internally about owner, support role, and intended actions.
detail: Kublai updated shared-context/hermes-kublai/EXECUTION_COORDINATION_PROTOCOL.md. Proposed operating rule: pre-exec handoff first for non-trivial/tool-using/shared work; simple chat can be answered directly; urgent safety can act first then summarize.

summary: Danny clarified that Telegram messages are not a reliable bot-to-bot channel. Hermes and Kublai must use the internal handoff system for coordination, not assume Kublai can read Hermes Telegram posts.
detail: Operational rule update: all bot-to-bot coordination must be written to shared-context/hermes-kublai/handoffs.jsonl or the append_handoff.py helper. Telegram mirrors are for Danny visibility only.

summary: Danny asked for brainstorming on making group chat coordination feel unified despite internal handoffs. Kublai will propose UX/protocol improvements in chat; Hermes can amend internally if it has additions.
detail: No automatic Telegram mirror. Proposed owner=kublai for concise brainstorm; support=hermes for later amendments. No state changes except this pre-exec handoff.

summary: Danny asked us to decide the best solution and execute it. Proposed final solution: internal-first coordination, one visible owner per request, shared group_state.json, no auto-mirrored handoff logs, concise combined updates only when useful.
detail: Kublai will implement shared-context/hermes-kublai/UNIFIED_CHAT_PROTOCOL.md and group_state.json. Hermes should use the same state file and internal handoffs before non-trivial work. Telegram remains human-facing; internal handoffs remain bot-to-bot.
